{"step_by_step_thinking": "Oxygen (O2) is a diatomic molecule with a symmetrical linear geometry, which means the bond dipoles cancel each other out, resulting in a molecule without a permanent dipole. Helium (He) is a monatomic molecule and does not have a dipole moment. Liquid nitrogen (N2) is also a diatomic molecule with a linear geometry, similar to oxygen, and thus does not have a permanent dipole. Water (H2O) has a bent molecular geometry with polar covalent bonds, leading to an uneven distribution of charge and a permanent dipole moment.", "answer_choice": "D"}
